item.title: 🇵🇰🇮🇷 Pakistan and Iran Leaders Discuss Regional Peace in Phone Call
item.description: Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif spoke with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian about regional stability as diplomatic efforts continue to resolve tensions with the US. The call comes amid uncertainty over whether US envoys will travel to Islamabad for talks.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇴🇲 Iran FM Travels to Oman After Sharing Diplomatic Framework in Pakistan
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrived in Oman from Islamabad where he presented a framework for ending the US-Iran conflict. Araghchi expressed skepticism about US commitment to diplomacy as Trump cancels envoy visit.

item.title: 🇲🇱⚔️ Coordinated Attacks Hit Multiple Cities Across Mali
item.description: Gunmen launched coordinated attacks against Malian military positions in the capital Bamako, Kati, Sevare, and northern areas in one of the largest assaults on the junta in years. The attacks targeted the international airport and military bases.

item.title: 🇭🇺🏛️ Hungary's Orban to Leave Parliament After Landslide Election Defeat
item.description: Viktor Orban announced he will give up his parliamentary mandate after losing to Peter Magyar in Hungary's election. The long-serving prime minister said he wants to stay on as Fidesz party leader while rebuilding the national side.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ Trump Renews Call to End Filibuster Over SAVE America Act Impasse
item.description: President Trump called for eliminating the filibuster to pass the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility Act, which remains stalled in the Senate. The push highlights ongoing legislative gridlock on election-related legislation.

item.title: 🇲🇱 Armed groups launch coordinated attacks across Mali
item.description: Explosions and gunfire reported in Bamako, Kati, Gao, Kidal, and Sevare as jihadist group JNIM and separatist Azawad Liberation Front stage simultaneous assaults. The UK Foreign Office has advised against all travel to Mali, and Bamako International Airport has been temporarily closed.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Macron says EU defense clause is stronger than NATO Article 5
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron declared during a visit to Greece that the EU mutual defense clause (Article 42.7) is stronger than NATO Article 5 because it leaves no option for non-compliance. The statement comes as Trump questions NATO and US commitment to European defense.

item.title: 🇻🇪 US eases sanctions to let Venezuela pay Maduro legal fees
item.description: The US Department of Justice modified sanctions to allow the Venezuelan government to cover legal fees for former president Nicolas Maduro, who faces federal drug trafficking charges in New York. Maduro defense lawyers had argued that blocking payment violated his right to counsel.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo brushes off Trump criticism during Africa tour
item.description: Pope Leo XIV said he will continue speaking out against war despite criticism from President Trump, who attacked the pontiff for opposing US Iran policy. The Pope began an 11-day tour of Africa amid escalating Vatican-Washington tensions over the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇨🇳 US senator urges China participation in nuclear arms control
item.description: Senator Deb Fischer, chair of the Senate Armed Services strategic forces subcommittee, said China must be involved in any nuclear arms control talks. China has repeatedly refused to join US-Russia nuclear agreements, seeking parity before negotiations.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump Cancels US Envoys' Trip to Pakistan for Iran Talks
item.description: President Donald Trump cancelled a planned trip by US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan for Iran war mediation talks, saying 'If they want to talk, all they have to do is call.' Iran's foreign minister had already left Islamabad, dealing a setback to peace prospects.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Republicans Retool Midterm Strategy Amid Iran War and Gas Prices
item.description: With US gas prices up and President Trump's approval ratings down as the Iran war drags on, Republicans are recalibrating their blueprint for November's midterm elections, focusing on Trump's policies while distancing from Trump himself.

item.title: 🇲🇽🇺🇸 Mexico Says US Agents Killed in Crash Weren't Authorized to Operate There
item.description: The Mexican government stated that two US federal agents - widely reported to be CIA officers - killed in a car crash during an anti-narcotics raid were not authorized to operate on Mexican territory. One had entered as a visitor, the other on a diplomatic passport.

item.title: 🇵🇸 Gaza's Deir el-Balah Holds First Municipal Elections in 20 Years
item.description: Residents of Deir el-Balah in central Gaza cast ballots in the first municipal elections since 2006, with voters expressing joy at participating in democratic processes after two decades. The elections come amid stalled ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇲🇲 Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi Meets Myanmar's Military Leader
item.description: China's top diplomat visited Myanmar's capital Naypyitaw and met with Min Aung Hlaing as part of a regional tour aimed at strengthening Beijing's political, security and strategic ties in Southeast Asia. The visit follows Min Aung Hlaing's swearing in as president on April 10.

item.title: 🇵🇰🇺🇸🇮🇷 Confusion over Iran talks as Trump cancels envoy trip to Pakistan
item.description: Iran says no direct talks planned with US despite Trump sending envoys Kushner and Witkoff to Pakistan for negotiations. Tehran foreign ministry denies any US discussions even as Araghchi arrives in Islamabad.

item.title: 🇵🇰 Islamabad bears the cost of Pakistan mediates US-Iran negotiations
item.description: Pakistani capital experiences semi-lockdown conditions as the country attempts to broker peace between Washington and Tehran. Next round of talks remains uncertain as daily life disrupted.

item.title: 🇺🇦💥 Eight killed in Russian mass strike on Dnipro and multiple cities
item.description: Russia launched one of its largest aerial attacks on Ukraine, killing at least 8 and injuring 60 across Dnipro and other cities. The assault involved missiles and drones targeting multiple locations overnight.

item.title: 🇨🇳⛓️ China tungsten export restrictions hit global hobby and defense supply chains
item.description: Beijing limits exports of tungsten, a critical material for both hobby manufacturing and defense industries. The restrictions come amid broader trade tensions and supply chain realignments.

item.title: 🛰️🌍 Low Earth orbit control could determine future world order, Chinese analysis warns
item.description: A Chinese strategic analysis highlights Starlink capabilities in conflict zones and warns of a new form of power that could allow or cut connectivity in real time, reshaping geopolitical leverage.

item.title: 🇫🇮🏊 Finland Joins Nordic Boycott of World Aquatics Over Russia Participation
item.description: Finland announced it will not host international swimming championships while World Aquatics allows Russian and Belarusian athletes to compete, joining Norway and Poland in a growing Nordic protest against the governing body.

item.title: 🇬🇧👑 King Charles III to Visit US Amid Iran War Tensions
item.description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla begin a four-day state visit to the US on Monday, seeking to strengthen US-UK ties despite tensions over Britain refusing to support the Iran war. The visit follows Queen Elizabeth IIs 1991 precedent of addressing Congress.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Rejects Direct Talks with US as Pakistan Mediation Continues
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi laid out Iran's demands in Islamabad while ruling out face-to-face meetings with US negotiators. The US has dispatched envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan for indirect peace talks.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Major Russian Attack Kills Seven in Ukraine as Dnipro Struck by Barrage
item.description: Russia launched over 660 drones and missiles in a massive overnight attack that killed at least seven people and wounded dozens in Dnipro and other Ukrainian regions. It marks one of the largest Russian barrages in recent weeks.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Four Killed in Israeli Strikes on Southern Lebanon Despite Ceasefire
item.description: Israeli strikes killed four people in southern Lebanon, state media reported, while the Israeli military said Hezbollah had fired rockets at Israel. The attacks come despite an extended ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon.
